lines.en_lit: ten sanctifications, However, the Rabbis nine are The Rabbis said to you: the final sanctification, when he removes sacred garments and dons non-sacred garments, he performs it there.

The Sages taught “And Aaron shall come into the Tent of Meeting” Why does he come He comes only to remove the spoon and the coal pan as the entire portion was stated in the order except for this verse, What is the reason
Rav Ḥisda said: We learned five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses and sanctifies on the day and if the order you find only three immersions and six sanctifications.

It was taught Rabbi Yehuda said: From where five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses and sanctifies on the day the verse states: “And Aaron shall come into the Tent of Meeting and he shall remove the linen garments And he shall wash his flesh in water in a sacred place and put on his garments, and he shall go out and perform this you learned: Anyone who moves from service, to service, requires immersion.

Rabbi said: From where five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses and sanctifies on the day which is stated: “He shall be dressed in a sacred linen tunic, and with linen trousers next to his flesh, and he shall be girded with a linen belt, and he shall wear a linen mitre; they are sacred garments, and he shall wash his flesh in water and then put them on” this you learned: Anyone who moves from service, to service requires immersion. And says: “They are sacred garments”; all of the garments are equated to one another.

And they are five services: daily morning offering, in golden garments; the service of the day, in white garments; his ram ram of the people in golden garments. spoon and coal pan in white garments. daily afternoon offering in golden garments
And from where that each and every immersion requires two sanctifications the verse states: and he shall remove And he shall wash “And he shall wash and put on

Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says a fortiori inference: Just as in a place where does not require immersion, requires sanctification in a place where requires immersion is it not right that it requires sanctification

If Just as there, one sanctification so too here, one sanctification the verse states: “And Aaron shall come into the Tent of Meeting and he shall remove the linen garments that he put on” What the verse states: “That he put on”? Does a person remove other that he put on? Rather, to equate removal to donning Just as donning requires sanctification, so too, removal requires sanctification.

Rabbi Yehuda said: From where five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses and sanctifies on the day the verse states: “And Aaron shall come into the Tent of Meeting And he shall wash his flesh in water in a sacred place” this you learned: Anyone who moves from service, to service, requires immersion. We found from white garments to golden garments; from where from golden garments to white garments?
